title: H2 JDBC INIT injection via unsanitized database details in EE serialization import
description: Metabase Enterprise's serialization import endpoint (POST /api/ee/serialization/import) lets an authenticated admin upload a crafted archive whose Database YAML contains arbitrary connection-detail keys. When the imported H2 database is synced, connection-details->spec :h2 passes the attacker-controlled details map straight into the H2 JDBC spec built by driver-api/spec. Because the map can carry an INIT property (and other low-level JDBC keys such as connection-uri/subname), H2 will execute the supplied SQL when the connection is opened, which Metabase uses to write and then run a Clojure payload for OS command execution, or to exfiltrate arbitrary files readable by the JVM. The H2 driver only stripped INIT from options embedded inside the :db connection string, never from top-level details keys.
